description_long: This apartment building consists of two tall, octagonal towers joined side by side, each featuring a series of stacked, repeating floors. The exterior is dominated by a grid of black and white geometric panels, with large, dark-tinted windows set into each face, providing a modern and somewhat imposing appearance. The uppermost levels are topped with mechanical structures and antennae, suggesting rooftop utility spaces. The facade is highly regular, with horizontal white bands separating each floor and vertical black columns accentuating the building's height. There are no visible balconies, and the windows are arranged in a strict, symmetrical pattern. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, with a focus on function and verticality.
description: A pair of tall, octagonal apartment towers with black and white geometric facades and large, dark windows, topped with rooftop mechanical structures. The design is modern, symmetrical, and emphasizes verticality.
description_view_0: This view shows the front faces of both towers, highlighting the white horizontal bands and black vertical columns, as well as the rooftop mechanical details.
description_view_1: From this angle, the side profiles of the towers are visible, emphasizing the octagonal shape and the regular window arrangement.
description_view_2: This view displays the rear faces of the towers, with the same geometric window pattern and prominent rooftop structures.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the towers is shown, again revealing the octagonal form and the consistent black and white facade.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a highly regular, symmetrical grid pattern, with each floor featuring evenly spaced, large rectangular windows on every face of the octagonal towers.
